title: Parallelize independent sub-reconcilers
summary: Review of changes addressing berendt's external review #1. Two items were addressed: (1) misleading comment claiming reconcileDatabase depends on the parallel group's results was corrected to accurately reflect the dependency graph (W-002), and (2) a new BenchmarkReconcile_FullReconcile_WithLatency benchmark was added that injects 5ms simulated API latency via interceptor.Funcs into every client operation, enabling meaningful wall-clock measurement of parallelization gains (W-001/I-001). All unit tests pass with -race, go vet is clean, and both benchmark variants run successfully. The golangci-lint SA1019 finding on shortestRequeue (line 214) is pre-existing and outside this diff's scope.

tests_checklist.item: All existing unit tests pass with -race (go test -race -count=1)
Warm-up reconcile validates setup correctness before benchmark loop

code_quality_checklist.item: Comment at keystone_controller.go:102-106 now accurately reflects dependency graph
Interceptor covers all client operations (Get, List, Create, Update, Patch, Delete, SubResourceUpdate)
No scope creep — only the two items from berendt's review are addressed

security_checklist.item: No security-relevant changes in this diff

architecture_checklist.item: Latency injection via interceptor.Funcs is the standard controller-runtime pattern
Benchmark variant complements (not replaces) the zero-latency baseline benchmark

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — benchmark reuses existing test helpers
No unnecessary additions beyond what was requested

fail_fast_checklist.item: Warm-up reconcile uses b.Fatalf on error for immediate feedback

defensive_checklist.item: Warm-up validates both error and non-zero result before benchmark loop

suggested_improvements: Consider documenting before/after benchmark results (BenchmarkReconcile_FullReconcile_WithLatency ns/op with parallel vs hypothetical sequential baseline) in the PR description to satisfy the 30% acceptance criterion with concrete numbers.
